notes.anchor: what was given to Lucius Caesar to take back to him
notes.note: L. Caesar (a young kinsman of the dictator) served as the formal go-between in the January 49 negotiations, carrying Caesar's demands to Pompey and the consuls and bringing their written replies back; the exchange is the diplomatic thread running through 7.16-7.19.

notes.anchor: if he himself comes into the Picenum country
notes.note: Picenum, the Adriatic-coast region north-east of Rome, was Pompey's family heartland and recruiting ground; his hope of rallying it explains his confidence that Cicero might soon return to Rome.

notes.anchor: We were ordered by the consuls to come to Capua on the Nones of February
notes.note: The consuls of 49 (Lentulus Crus and C. Marcellus) summoned the senators to Capua, the de facto Pompeian command-centre in Campania, for the fifth of February (the February Nones), to coordinate the war effort.
